name: app.R
content:
# =============================================================================
#  APPLE FINANCIAL DETECTIVE — An interactive scavenger hunt (Shiny app)
#  Based on: "Apple Financial Statements: What Can They Tell Us?"
#  Data: Apple Inc. FY2025 Form 10-K (Statements of Operations, Balance
#        Sheets, Statements of Cash Flows)
#
#  How students play (one question at a time, 20 questions):
#   1. Read the "case" (question) and click the filing cabinet where the
#      information lives: Income Statement / Balance Sheet / Cash Flow
#      Statement / Not in the statements.
#   2. If the info exists, they must then FIND and TYPE the actual number.
#   3. If the info does NOT exist, they get a quick "Why not?" reflection
#      challenge (this covers Part B of the paper exercise).
#   Feedback, hints, streaks, points and fun facts keep the flow engaging.
#
#  Run with:  shiny::runApp("app.R")   (requires only the shiny package)
# =============================================================================

library(shiny)

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 1. QUESTION BANK  (all $ figures in millions, per the 10-K)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# sources_ok : all statements that count as correct (some items appear twice)
# primary    : the statement we highlight in feedback
# answer     : numeric value(s) in $ millions (NULL for "NO" questions)
# whynot_*   : mini-challenge for questions whose answer is NOT available
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

QUESTIONS <- list(
  list(id = 1,
       q = "What were Apple's total net sales in fiscal year 2025?",
       sources_ok = "IS", primary = "IS",
       answer = 416161, n_needed = 1,
       hint_source = "Sales (revenue) is the very first thing a company reports when it summarizes a year of business performance.",
       hint_number = "Look at the top of the Income Statement — 'Total net sales' for the year ended September 27, 2025.",
       explain = "Total net sales live at the TOP of the Income Statement — that's why revenue is nicknamed the 'top line'. Apple sold $416,161 million ($416.2 billion) of products and services in FY2025.",
       funfact = "That's more than $1.1 billion of sales every single day — including weekends!"),

  list(id = 2,
       q = "How much cash did Apple have on September 27, 2025?",
       sources_ok = c("BS", "CF"), primary = "BS",
       answer = 35934, n_needed = 1,
       hint_source = "Cash is something a company OWNS on a specific date. Which statement is a snapshot of what the company owns and owes on one day?",
       hint_number = "It's the very first line under Current assets. (Bonus: the Cash Flow Statement's ending balance shows the same number!)",
       explain = "Cash is an ASSET measured at a point in time, so it sits on the Balance Sheet ($35,934 million as of Sept 27, 2025). Neat cross-check: the Cash Flow Statement's ending cash balance shows the identical figure — the statements tie together!",
       funfact = "Apple's cash pile alone is bigger than the entire market value of many Fortune 500 companies."),

  list(id = 3,
       q = "How much cash did Apple have on January 15, 2025?",
       sources_ok = "NO", primary = "NO",
       answer = NULL, n_needed = 0,
       hint_source = "Check the DATES printed at the top of each statement. Do any of them mention January?",
       hint_number = NULL,
       explain = "Financial statements are prepared only for specific reporting dates — Apple's fiscal year ends in late September. The Balance Sheet is a snapshot of Sept 27, 2025 (and Sept 28, 2024). There is simply no snapshot taken on January 15, so this number does not exist in these statements.",
       funfact = NULL,
       whynot_correct = "Statements are snapshots at fiscal period-end dates only — no snapshot exists for a random mid-year date.",
       whynot_options = c(
         "Statements are snapshots at fiscal period-end dates only — no snapshot exists for a random mid-year date.",
         "Apple is legally forbidden from counting its cash in January.",
         "Cash balances are a trade secret and never disclosed."),
       whynot_valuable = "Investors might love a daily cash figure, but accounting works in reporting periods — quarterly and annual snapshots — not a live ticker."),

  list(id = 4,
       q = "How much did Apple owe to its suppliers (accounts payable)?",
       sources_ok = "BS", primary = "BS",
       answer = 69860, n_needed = 1,
       hint_source = "Money OWED to suppliers is a debt — an obligation. Which statement lists what the company owes?",
       hint_number = "Look under Current liabilities — it's the first line there.",
       explain = "Accounts payable is a LIABILITY (money owed) at a point in time, so it appears on the Balance Sheet: $69,860 million owed to suppliers as of Sept 27, 2025.",
       funfact = "Apple famously negotiates long payment terms — suppliers effectively help finance Apple's operations for free!"),

  list(id = 5,
       q = "How much did Apple spend on Research & Development in 2025?",
       sources_ok = "IS", primary = "IS",
       answer = 34550, n_needed = 1,
       hint_source = "R&D is an EXPENSE of running the business during the year. Where do expenses get subtracted from revenue?",
       hint_number = "Look under Operating expenses on the Income Statement.",
       explain = "R&D is an operating EXPENSE of the period, so it belongs on the Income Statement: $34,550 million in FY2025. Expenses reduce profit, and the Income Statement is the story of revenues minus expenses.",
       funfact = "Apple's R&D budget is larger than the GDP of some countries — that's the price of inventing the next big thing."),

  list(id = 6,
       q = "What was Apple's net income in 2025?",
       sources_ok = c("IS", "CF"), primary = "IS",
       answer = 112010, n_needed = 1,
       hint_source = "Net income is the famous 'bottom line' — the final result of a year of revenues and expenses.",
       hint_number = "Scan to the bottom of the Income Statement. (It's also the first line of the Cash Flow Statement's operating section!)",
       explain = "Net income is the BOTTOM LINE of the Income Statement: $112,010 million. Fun connection: the Cash Flow Statement also starts with this same net income and adjusts it to arrive at actual cash generated.",
       funfact = "$112 billion of profit ≈ over $212,000 of profit per MINUTE, all year long."),

  list(id = 7,
       q = "What is Apple's current stock price?",
       sources_ok = "NO", primary = "NO",
       answer = NULL, n_needed = 0,
       hint_source = "Who sets the stock price — Apple's accountants, or buyers and sellers in the stock market?",
       hint_number = NULL,
       explain = "Stock price is determined minute-by-minute by investors trading in the MARKET — it is not an accounting figure. Financial statements report the company's own transactions and balances, not what the market thinks the shares are worth.",
       funfact = NULL,
       whynot_correct = "The stock price is set by the market in real time — it's not an accounting record of the company's transactions.",
       whynot_options = c(
         "The stock price is set by the market in real time — it's not an accounting record of the company's transactions.",
         "Apple hides the stock price so employees won't sell their shares.",
         "The stock price is in the statements, but written in a secret code."),
       whynot_valuable = "Hugely valuable to investors — but you find it on a stock exchange, not in the 10-K's financial statements."),

  list(id = 8,
       q = "How many iPhones did Apple sell in 2025?",
       sources_ok = "NO", primary = "NO",
       answer = NULL, n_needed = 0,
       hint_source = "The statements show DOLLARS of product sales. Do you see anywhere that counts UNITS of anything?",
       hint_number = NULL,
       explain = "The Income Statement reports sales in DOLLARS ($307,003M of Products revenue), not in units. Apple stopped disclosing unit sales back in 2018. Unit counts are operational data that companies may choose to share elsewhere — or not at all.",
       funfact = NULL,
       whynot_correct = "Statements report dollar amounts, not unit counts — and Apple chooses not to disclose unit sales anymore.",
       whynot_options = c(
         "Statements report dollar amounts, not unit counts — and Apple chooses not to disclose unit sales anymore.",
         "Apple lost count of how many iPhones it sold.",
         "Unit sales are only reported in the Balance Sheet's footnotes."),
       whynot_valuable = "Analysts would love this number! Apple keeps it private partly so competitors (and critics) can't track volume trends."),

  list(id = 9,
       q = "How much revenue did Apple earn from Services in 2025?",
       sources_ok = "IS", primary = "IS",
       answer = 109158, n_needed = 1,
       hint_source = "Revenue earned during the year — which statement breaks revenue into its parts?",
       hint_number = "The Income Statement splits Net sales into Products and Services. You want the Services line for 2025.",
       explain = "The Income Statement breaks total revenue into Products and Services. Services (App Store, iCloud, Apple Music, etc.) brought in $109,158 million — more than a quarter of Apple's total sales!",
       funfact = "Apple's Services business alone would rank among the biggest companies in the world if it stood on its own."),

  list(id = 10,
       q = "What was Apple's net income for October 2025 alone?",
       sources_ok = "NO", primary = "NO",
       answer = NULL, n_needed = 0,
       hint_source = "These statements cover a full FISCAL YEAR. Can you slice a single month out of them?",
       hint_number = NULL,
       explain = "These are ANNUAL statements covering the year ended September 27, 2025. October 2025 isn't even inside that period — and even months that ARE inside the year are never broken out separately. The finest slice public companies report is a quarter.",
       funfact = NULL,
       whynot_correct = "Statements cover defined reporting periods (year/quarter) — single months are never broken out, and Oct 2025 is outside this fiscal year anyway.",
       whynot_options = c(
         "Statements cover defined reporting periods (year/quarter) — single months are never broken out, and Oct 2025 is outside this fiscal year anyway.",
         "October results are only published in Apple's employee newsletter.",
         "Companies aren't allowed to earn income in October."),
       whynot_valuable = "Monthly detail exists internally for managers, but public reporting works in quarters and years."),

  list(id = 11,
       q = "How much did Apple pay in dividends in 2025?",
       sources_ok = "CF", primary = "CF",
       answer = 15421, n_needed = 1,
       hint_source = "Dividends are CASH leaving the company and going to shareholders. Which statement tracks cash moving in and out?",
       hint_number = "Look in the Financing activities section of the Cash Flow Statement.",
       explain = "Dividends paid are a FINANCING cash outflow, shown on the Cash Flow Statement: $15,421 million paid to shareholders in FY2025. (Careful — dividends are NOT an expense, so they never appear on the Income Statement!)",
       funfact = "Common trap avoided: many students look for dividends on the Income Statement. They're a distribution of profit, not a cost of earning it."),

  list(id = 12,
       q = "What is the total value of Apple's assets?",
       sources_ok = "BS", primary = "BS",
       answer = 359241, n_needed = 1,
       hint_source = "'Assets' is literally one of the two halves of one statement's famous equation: Assets = Liabilities + Equity.",
       hint_number = "Find the 'Total assets' line — it's where the assets section ends.",
       explain = "Total assets belong to the Balance Sheet: $359,241 million as of Sept 27, 2025. And check the magic: Total liabilities ($285,508M) + Shareholders' equity ($73,733M) = exactly $359,241M. The Balance Sheet always balances!",
       funfact = "Surprise: Apple's assets ($359B) are much smaller than its annual sales ($416B) — Apple is an asset-light machine."),

  list(id = 13,
       q = "What is Tim Cook's (Apple's CEO) salary?",
       sources_ok = "NO", primary = "NO",
       answer = NULL, n_needed = 0,
       hint_source = "The statements show TOTAL company-wide amounts. Do they ever name individual people and what they earn?",
       hint_number = NULL,
       explain = "Financial statements aggregate everything — all salaries are bundled inside expense totals like SG&A. Individual executive pay IS publicly disclosed, but in a different document: the annual proxy statement (DEF 14A), not the three core financial statements.",
       funfact = NULL,
       whynot_correct = "Statements show aggregated totals; individual executive pay is disclosed in the proxy statement, a separate document.",
       whynot_options = c(
         "Statements show aggregated totals; individual executive pay is disclosed in the proxy statement, a separate document.",
         "CEO salaries are confidential by law and never disclosed anywhere.",
         "Tim Cook works for free, so there is nothing to report."),
       whynot_valuable = "Investors do care — that's why the SEC requires it in the proxy statement. Just not in these three statements."),

  list(id = 14,
       q = "How much income tax did Apple ACTUALLY PAY IN CASH in 2025?",
       sources_ok = "CF", primary = "CF",
       answer = 43369, n_needed = 1,
       hint_source = "Careful — this is a classic trap! The Income Statement shows the tax EXPENSE. But the question asks about cash actually PAID. Which statement tracks real cash?",
       hint_number = "Scroll to the very bottom of the Cash Flow Statement — the 'Supplemental cash flow disclosure'.",
       explain = "Trap question! The Income Statement's 'Provision for income taxes' ($20,719M) is the accounting EXPENSE. Cash actually PAID appears at the bottom of the Cash Flow Statement: $43,369 million — more than double the expense! Timing differences and one-off payments make accrual accounting and cash reality very different things.",
       funfact = "This gap is exactly why the Cash Flow Statement exists: profit is an opinion, cash is a fact."),

  list(id = 15,
       q = "Did Apple make more profit in 2025 than in 2024? Find BOTH net income numbers.",
       sources_ok = "IS", primary = "IS",
       answer = c(112010, 93736), n_needed = 2,
       hint_source = "Profit for the year = net income. Which statement shows it — and helpfully shows several years side by side?",
       hint_number = "The Income Statement shows three years in columns. Grab the bottom line for 2025 AND for 2024.",
       explain = "The Income Statement conveniently shows three years side by side. Net income was $112,010M in 2025 vs $93,736M in 2024 — so yes, profit grew by about $18 billion (roughly +19%). Comparative columns exist precisely so you can spot trends.",
       funfact = "Interesting twist: 2024 profit was lower partly because of a one-time ~$10B European tax charge. Always ask WHY a number moved."),

  list(id = 16,
       q = "How much long-term (non-current) debt does Apple owe?",
       sources_ok = "BS", primary = "BS",
       answer = 78328, n_needed = 1,
       hint_source = "Debt owed is a liability, and 'how much do they owe RIGHT NOW' is a snapshot question.",
       hint_number = "Look under Non-current liabilities for 'Term debt'.",
       explain = "Long-term borrowings sit in the Non-current liabilities section of the Balance Sheet: $78,328 million of term debt due beyond one year. (The $12,350M of term debt due within a year is listed separately under Current liabilities.)",
       funfact = "Why does cash-rich Apple borrow at all? Debt is cheap for Apple and helps fund buybacks and dividends efficiently."),

  list(id = 17,
       q = "What will Apple's revenue be next year (FY2026)?",
       sources_ok = "NO", primary = "NO",
       answer = NULL, n_needed = 0,
       hint_source = "Financial statements are a record of things that already HAPPENED. Is next year's revenue a fact yet?",
       hint_number = NULL,
       explain = "Financial statements are HISTORICAL — they report transactions that have already occurred and been audited. Forecasts of the future are not facts; they're estimates, and they live in analyst reports and management commentary, never in the audited statements.",
       funfact = NULL,
       whynot_correct = "Statements are historical records of what already happened — the future hasn't happened, so it can't be reported.",
       whynot_options = c(
         "Statements are historical records of what already happened — the future hasn't happened, so it can't be reported.",
         "FY2026 revenue is printed on the last page, in invisible ink.",
         "Auditors ban companies from ever discussing the future."),
       whynot_valuable = "Investors care about the future MOST of all — which is why forecasting is a whole profession. But audited statements only report verified history."),

  list(id = 18,
       q = "How much inventory did Apple hold on September 27, 2025?",
       sources_ok = "BS", primary = "BS",
       answer = 5718, n_needed = 1,
       hint_source = "Inventory is stuff the company OWNS (products waiting to be sold) on a specific date.",
       hint_number = "Look under Current assets for 'Inventories'.",
       explain = "Inventory is an ASSET held at a point in time → Balance Sheet: just $5,718 million. That's remarkably tiny for a company selling $416B a year — Apple's supply chain turns inventory over in a matter of days.",
       funfact = "Tim Cook (a supply-chain legend) once compared inventory to dairy: 'You want to manage it like you're in the milk business — if it gets past its freshness date, you have a problem.'"),

  list(id = 19,
       q = "Who are Apple's biggest customers?",
       sources_ok = "NO", primary = "NO",
       answer = NULL, n_needed = 0,
       hint_source = "Scan the statements — do you see any customer NAMES anywhere, or only totals?",
       hint_number = NULL,
       explain = "The statements aggregate ALL customers into total revenue — no names appear. (Companies must flag it in the notes if a single customer exceeds 10% of revenue, but Apple sells to hundreds of millions of customers, so none comes close.)",
       funfact = NULL,
       whynot_correct = "Statements aggregate all customers into totals — individual customer identities aren't part of the financial statements.",
       whynot_options = c(
         "Statements aggregate all customers into totals — individual customer identities aren't part of the financial statements.",
         "Apple's biggest customer is listed on the Balance Sheet under Assets.",
         "Customer names are removed to protect them from paparazzi."),
       whynot_valuable = "Competitors and analysts would find this fascinating — which is partly why aggregate reporting protects commercially sensitive detail."),

  list(id = 20,
       q = "How much did Apple spend buying back its own stock in 2025?",
       sources_ok = "CF", primary = "CF",
       answer = 90711, n_needed = 1,
       hint_source = "Buying back shares means CASH flowing out to shareholders — a transaction with the company's owners/financiers.",
       hint_number = "Check the Financing activities section: 'Repurchases of common stock'.",
       explain = "Share buybacks are a FINANCING cash outflow on the Cash Flow Statement: a staggering $90,711 million in FY2025. Like dividends, buybacks return cash to shareholders, so they belong in financing activities — never on the Income Statement.",
       funfact = "Apple spent MORE on buybacks ($90.7B) than most companies EARN. Buybacks + dividends together returned over $106B to shareholders in one year.")
)

N_Q <- length(QUESTIONS)

SOURCE_LABELS <- c(
  IS = "Income Statement",
  BS = "Balance Sheet",
  CF = "Cash Flow Statement",
  NO = "Not in the statements"
)
SOURCE_ICONS <- c(IS = "\U0001F4C8", BS = "\U00002696\UFE0F",
                  CF = "\U0001F4B5", NO = "\U0001F6AB")
SOURCE_TAGLINE <- c(
  IS = "Performance over the YEAR: revenues \u2212 expenses = profit",
  BS = "Snapshot on ONE DATE: what we own & owe",
  CF = "Cash moving IN and OUT during the year",
  NO = "This info lives somewhere else (or nowhere!)"
)

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 2. HELPERS
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

fmt_millions <- function(x) paste0("$", formatC(x, format = "d", big.mark = ","), " million")

# Extract every number a student typed ("$416,161", "416161 million", "112,010 and 93,736")
parse_numbers <- function(txt) {
  txt <- gsub(",", "", txt)
  m <- gregexpr("-?[0-9]+\\.?[0-9]*", txt)
  nums <- suppressWarnings(as.numeric(unlist(regmatches(txt, m))))
  nums[!is.na(nums)]
}

# Accept the value in millions (416161), billions (416.161 / 416.2), or raw dollars
matches_answer <- function(entered, target_millions) {
  if (length(entered) == 0) return(FALSE)
  entered <- abs(entered); target <- abs(target_millions)
  close <- function(a, b, tol) abs(a - b) <= tol * b
  any(close(entered, target,        0.001) |   # in millions (exact-ish)
      close(entered, target / 1000, 0.01 ) |   # in billions (allow rounding, e.g. 416.2)
      close(entered, target * 1e6,  0.001))    # typed full dollars
}

check_number_answer <- function(txt, targets) {
  nums <- parse_numbers(txt)
  all(vapply(targets, function(t) matches_answer(nums, t), logical(1)))
}

praise <- function() sample(c("Brilliant detective work!", "Nailed it!", "Sharp eyes!",
                              "Exactly right!", "You're on fire!", "Spot on!",
                              "Textbook-perfect!", "Outstanding!"), 1)
encourage <- function() sample(c("Not quite — but good detectives follow the clues.",
                                 "Close, but let's think it through.",
                                 "Hmm, that cabinet is empty. Try the hint!",
                                 "Not that one — here's a clue to redirect you."), 1)

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 3. UI
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

ui <- fluidPage(
  tags$head(tags$style(HTML("
    body { background: linear-gradient(160deg,#f5f7fb 0%,#e8eef7 100%);
           font-family: 'Segoe UI', 'Helvetica Neue', Arial, sans-serif; }
    .game-wrap { max-width: 860px; margin: 0 auto; padding: 18px 10px 60px; }
    .hdr { text-align:center; margin-bottom: 6px; }
    .hdr h1 { font-weight: 800; color:#1d2a44; font-size: 30px; margin-bottom: 2px;}
    .hdr p  { color:#5b6b85; margin-top: 0;}
    .scorebar { display:flex; justify-content:space-between; align-items:center;
                background:#ffffff; border-radius:14px; padding:10px 18px;
                box-shadow:0 2px 10px rgba(30,50,90,.08); margin-bottom:14px;
                font-weight:600; color:#33415c;}
    .scorebar .pill { background:#eef3fb; border-radius:20px; padding:4px 14px; }
    .progress-outer { height:10px; background:#dde5f0; border-radius:6px;
                      overflow:hidden; margin: 4px 0 18px;}
    .progress-inner { height:100%; background:linear-gradient(90deg,#4f8ef7,#38c98f);
                      transition: width .5s ease; }
    .case-card { background:#fff; border-radius:18px; padding:26px 28px;
                 box-shadow:0 6px 24px rgba(30,50,90,.10); }
    .case-no { font-size:13px; letter-spacing:2px; color:#8aa0c0;
               text-transform:uppercase; font-weight:700;}
    .case-q { font-size:22px; font-weight:700; color:#1d2a44; margin:6px 0 18px;
              line-height:1.35;}
    .cabinet-row { display:grid; grid-template-columns:1fr 1fr; gap:12px; }
    @media (max-width:640px){ .cabinet-row { grid-template-columns:1fr; } }
    .cabinet .btn { width:100%; text-align:left; background:#f7f9fd;
                    border:2px solid #e2e9f4; border-radius:14px; padding:14px 16px;
                    white-space:normal; transition:all .15s ease; }
    .cabinet .btn:hover { border-color:#4f8ef7; background:#eef4ff;
                          transform:translateY(-2px);
                          box-shadow:0 4px 14px rgba(79,142,247,.18);}
    .cab-title { font-weight:700; color:#1d2a44; font-size:16px; }
    .cab-sub { color:#6d7f9b; font-size:12.5px; margin-top:2px; }
    .fb { border-radius:14px; padding:16px 18px; margin-top:16px; font-size:15px;
          line-height:1.5; }
    .fb-good { background:#e9f9f0; border:1.5px solid #38c98f; color:#14532d; }
    .fb-bad  { background:#fdeeee; border:1.5px solid #ef7d7d; color:#7f1d1d; }
    .fb-hint { background:#fff8e6; border:1.5px solid #f2c94c; color:#7a5c00; }
    .fb-info { background:#eef4ff; border:1.5px solid #7fa8f0; color:#1e3a8a; }
    .fb b { font-weight:700; }
    .funfact { margin-top:10px; padding:10px 14px; background:#f3efff;
               border-left:4px solid #8b5cf6; border-radius:8px;
               color:#4c1d95; font-size:14px;}
    .num-zone { margin-top:18px; padding:18px; background:#f7f9fd;
                border-radius:14px; border:1.5px dashed #b9c9e2; }
    .num-zone label { font-weight:700; color:#33415c; }
    .btn-go { background:linear-gradient(90deg,#4f8ef7,#38c98f); color:#fff;
              font-weight:700; border:none; border-radius:10px;
              padding:9px 22px; }
    .btn-go:hover { color:#fff; opacity:.92; }
    .btn-hint { background:#fff3cd; border:1.5px solid #f2c94c; color:#7a5c00;
                border-radius:10px; font-weight:600; }
    .btn-next { background:#1d2a44; color:#fff; border-radius:10px;
                font-weight:700; padding:9px 26px; margin-top:14px;}
    .btn-next:hover { color:#fff; opacity:.9; }
    .streak { color:#e2574c; font-weight:800; }
    .whynot-opt .btn { width:100%; text-align:left; background:#fff;
                       border:2px solid #e2e9f4; border-radius:12px;
                       padding:12px 14px; margin-bottom:8px; white-space:normal;
                       color:#33415c; font-size:14.5px;}
    .whynot-opt .btn:hover { border-color:#8b5cf6; background:#f8f5ff; }
    .final-stat { display:inline-block; background:#fff; border-radius:14px;
                  padding:14px 22px; margin:6px; box-shadow:0 3px 12px rgba(30,50,90,.08);
                  text-align:center; }
    .final-stat .big { font-size:26px; font-weight:800; color:#1d2a44; }
    .final-stat .lbl { font-size:12px; color:#6d7f9b; text-transform:uppercase;
                       letter-spacing:1px;}
    .review-row { padding:8px 12px; border-bottom:1px solid #eef1f7; font-size:14px;}
    .badge-big { font-size:46px; }
    .start-card { text-align:center; }
    .start-card ul { text-align:left; display:inline-block; color:#44546f;
                     line-height:1.7;}
  "))),
  div(class = "game-wrap",
      div(class = "hdr",
          h1("\U0001F50E Apple Financial Detective"),
          p("A scavenger hunt through Apple's FY2025 financial statements")),
      uiOutput("game_ui")
  )
)

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 4. SERVER
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

server <- function(input, output, session) {

  rv <- reactiveValues(
    stage      = "start",   # start | source | number | whynot | reveal | done
    idx        = 1,
    score      = 0,
    streak     = 0,
    best_streak= 0,
    attempts   = 0,         # wrong source attempts this question
    num_attempts = 0,       # wrong number attempts this question
    hint_used  = FALSE,
    feedback   = NULL,      # list(type=, html=)
    got_source_right = NA,
    whynot_order = NULL,
    results    = list()     # per-question log
  )

  cur <- reactive(QUESTIONS[[rv$idx]])

  add_result <- function(source_ok, number_ok = NA) {
    q <- cur()
    rv$results[[length(rv$results) + 1]] <- list(
      id = q$id, q = q$q, primary = q$primary,
      source_ok = source_ok, number_ok = number_ok)
  }

  advance <- function() {
    rv$attempts <- 0; rv$num_attempts <- 0; rv$hint_used <- FALSE
    rv$feedback <- NULL; rv$got_source_right <- NA; rv$whynot_order <- NULL
    if (rv$idx < N_Q) { rv$idx <- rv$idx + 1; rv$stage <- "source"
    } else rv$stage <- "done"
  }

  # ---- Statement cabinet clicks -------------------------------------------
  lapply(names(SOURCE_LABELS), function(src) {
    observeEvent(input[[paste0("pick_", src)]], {
      req(rv$stage == "source")
      q <- cur()
      if (src %in% q$sources_ok) {
        # ---------- correct source ----------
        rv$streak <- rv$streak + 1
        rv$best_streak <- max(rv$best_streak, rv$streak)
        pts <- if (rv$attempts == 0) 10 else 5
        if (rv$hint_used) pts <- max(pts - 2, 3)
        rv$score <- rv$score + pts
        rv$got_source_right <- (rv$attempts == 0)
        also <- setdiff(q$sources_ok, src)
        also_txt <- if (length(also))
          paste0(" (It also shows up on the ", SOURCE_LABELS[also],
                 " \u2014 the statements are connected!)") else ""
        streak_txt <- if (rv$streak >= 3)
          paste0(" <span class='streak'>\U0001F525 ", rv$streak,
                 " in a row!</span>") else ""
        if (q$primary == "NO") {
          rv$feedback <- list(type = "good", html = paste0(
            "<b>", praise(), " +", pts, " pts.</b>", streak_txt,
            " You're right \u2014 this information is <b>not</b> in the three statements.",
            "<br><br>", q$explain))
          rv$whynot_order <- sample(seq_along(q$whynot_options))
          rv$stage <- "whynot"
        } else {
          rv$feedback <- list(type = "good", html = paste0(
            "<b>", praise(), " +", pts, " pts.</b>", streak_txt,
            " Yes \u2014 the <b>", SOURCE_LABELS[q$primary], "</b> is the place.",
            also_txt,
            "<br><br>\U0001F3AF <b>Level 2:</b> now open that statement and find the actual number!"))
          rv$stage <- "number"
        }
      } else {
        # ---------- wrong source ----------
        rv$streak <- 0
        rv$attempts <- rv$attempts + 1
        if (rv$attempts == 1) {
          rv$feedback <- list(type = "bad", html = paste0(
            "<b>", encourage(), "</b> The <b>", SOURCE_LABELS[src],
            "</b> doesn't hold this one.<br><br>\U0001F4A1 <i>Clue:</i> ",
            q$hint_source, "<br><br>Try another cabinet!"))
        } else {
          # reveal after 2 misses
          add_result(source_ok = FALSE,
                     number_ok = if (q$primary == "NO") NA else FALSE)
          reveal_html <- paste0(
            "<b>The answer was: ", SOURCE_ICONS[q$primary], " ",
            SOURCE_LABELS[q$primary], ".</b><br><br>", q$explain)
          if (q$primary != "NO")
            reveal_html <- paste0(reveal_html, "<br><br>\U0001F4CC The number: <b>",
              paste(vapply(q$answer, fmt_millions, ""), collapse = " and "), "</b>.")
          if (!is.null(q$funfact))
            reveal_html <- paste0(reveal_html,
              "<div class='funfact'>\U0001F4A1 ", q$funfact, "</div>")
          rv$feedback <- list(type = "info", html = reveal_html)
          rv$stage <- "reveal"
        }
      }
    })
  })

  # ---- Hint button on source stage ----------------------------------------
  observeEvent(input$hint_btn, {
    req(rv$stage == "source")
    rv$hint_used <- TRUE
    rv$feedback <- list(type = "hint",
      html = paste0("\U0001F4A1 <b>Detective's clue</b> (small point cost): ",
                    cur()$hint_source))
  })

  # ---- Number submission ---------------------------------------------------
  observeEvent(input$submit_num, {
    req(rv$stage == "number")
    q <- cur()
    ok <- check_number_answer(input$num_answer %||% "", q$answer)
    if (ok) {
      pts <- if (rv$num_attempts == 0) 10 else 5
      rv$score <- rv$score + pts
      add_result(source_ok = rv$got_source_right, number_ok = TRUE)
      html <- paste0("<b>", praise(), " +", pts, " pts.</b> The number is <b>",
                     paste(vapply(q$answer, fmt_millions, ""), collapse = " and "),
                     "</b>.<br><br>", q$explain)
      if (!is.null(q$funfact))
        html <- paste0(html, "<div class='funfact'>\U0001F4A1 ", q$funfact, "</div>")
      rv$feedback <- list(type = "good", html = html)
      rv$stage <- "reveal"
    } else {
      rv$num_attempts <- rv$num_attempts + 1
      if (rv$num_attempts == 1) {
        rv$feedback <- list(type = "hint", html = paste0(
          "<b>Not the number I have on file.</b><br><br>\U0001F4A1 <i>Where to look:</i> ",
          q$hint_number,
          "<br><br><i>Tip: amounts are in $ millions \u2014 you can type</i> 416161 <i>or</i> 416,161 <i>or</i> 416.2 billion."))
      } else {
        add_result(source_ok = rv$got_source_right, number_ok = FALSE)
        html <- paste0("<b>The number is ",
                       paste(vapply(q$answer, fmt_millions, ""), collapse = " and "),
                       ".</b><br><br>", q$explain)
        if (!is.null(q$funfact))
          html <- paste0(html, "<div class='funfact'>\U0001F4A1 ", q$funfact, "</div>")
        rv$feedback <- list(type = "info", html = html)
        rv$stage <- "reveal"
      }
    }
  })

  # ---- Why-not mini challenge ---------------------------------------------
  lapply(1:3, function(i) {
    observeEvent(input[[paste0("whynot_", i)]], {
      req(rv$stage == "whynot")
      q <- cur()
      chosen <- q$whynot_options[rv$whynot_order[i]]
      if (identical(chosen, q$whynot_correct)) {
        rv$score <- rv$score + 5
        add_result(source_ok = rv$got_source_right, number_ok = NA)
        rv$feedback <- list(type = "good", html = paste0(
          "<b>Exactly! +5 bonus pts.</b> ", q$whynot_correct,
          "<div class='funfact'>\U0001F9E0 <b>Is it valuable to investors?</b> ",
          q$whynot_valuable, "</div>"))
        rv$stage <- "reveal"
      } else {
        add_result(source_ok = rv$got_source_right, number_ok = NA)
        rv$feedback <- list(type = "info", html = paste0(
          "<b>Tempting, but no.</b> The real reason: ", q$whynot_correct,
          "<div class='funfact'>\U0001F9E0 <b>Is it valuable to investors?</b> ",
          q$whynot_valuable, "</div>"))
        rv$stage <- "reveal"
      }
    })
  })

  observeEvent(input$start_btn, { rv$stage <- "source" })
  observeEvent(input$next_btn,  { advance() })
  observeEvent(input$restart_btn, {
    rv$stage <- "start"; rv$idx <- 1; rv$score <- 0; rv$streak <- 0
    rv$best_streak <- 0; rv$attempts <- 0; rv$num_attempts <- 0
    rv$hint_used <- FALSE; rv$feedback <- NULL; rv$results <- list()
  })

  `%||%` <- function(a, b) if (is.null(a)) b else a

  # ---- UI builders ----------------------------------------------------------
  cabinet_btn <- function(src) {
    div(class = "cabinet",
        actionButton(paste0("pick_", src), label = HTML(paste0(
          "<div class='cab-title'>", SOURCE_ICONS[src], "  ",
          SOURCE_LABELS[src], "</div><div class='cab-sub'>",
          SOURCE_TAGLINE[src], "</div>"))))
  }

  feedback_box <- function() {
    fb <- rv$feedback
    if (is.null(fb)) return(NULL)
    cls <- switch(fb$type, good = "fb fb-good", bad = "fb fb-bad",
                  hint = "fb fb-hint", info = "fb fb-info")
    div(class = cls, HTML(fb$html))
  }

  output$game_ui <- renderUI({

    # ---------------- START SCREEN ----------------
    if (rv$stage == "start") {
      return(div(class = "case-card start-card",
        div(class = "badge-big", "\U0001F575\UFE0F\u200D\u2640\UFE0F"),
        h3("Your mission, detective"),
        p("Apple has handed you its three FY2025 financial statements:"),
        tags$ul(
          tags$li(HTML("\U0001F4C8 <b>Income Statement</b> \u2014 the year's performance story")),
          tags$li(HTML("\U00002696\UFE0F <b>Balance Sheet</b> \u2014 a snapshot of what Apple owns & owes")),
          tags$li(HTML("\U0001F4B5 <b>Cash Flow Statement</b> \u2014 where the cash came from and went"))),
        p(HTML("You'll get <b>20 cases</b>, one at a time. For each one:<br>
                <b>Level 1</b> \u2014 click the statement where the info lives (or call its bluff: some info isn't there at all!)<br>
                <b>Level 2</b> \u2014 hunt down the actual number in your printed statements and type it in.")),
        p(HTML("\U0001F525 Build streaks, earn points, dodge the trap questions.<br>
                <i>Keep Apple's statements next to you \u2014 you'll need them!</i>")),
        actionButton("start_btn", "\U0001F680 Start the investigation",
                     class = "btn-go", style = "font-size:17px;")))
    }

    # ---------------- FINAL SCREEN ----------------
    if (rv$stage == "done") {
      res <- rv$results
      n_src_right <- sum(vapply(res, function(r) isTRUE(r$source_ok), logical(1)))
      badge <- if (rv$score >= 350) "\U0001F3C6 Chief Financial Detective"
        else if (rv$score >= 260)   "\U0001F948 Senior Auditor"
        else if (rv$score >= 170)   "\U0001F949 Junior Analyst"
        else                        "\U0001F4DA Detective in Training"
      review <- lapply(res, function(r) {
        icon <- if (isTRUE(r$source_ok)) "\u2705"
                else if (isFALSE(r$source_ok)) "\u274C" else "\u2796"
        div(class = "review-row", HTML(paste0(
          icon, " <b>Q", r$id, "</b> \u2014 ", r$q,
          " <span style='color:#8aa0c0;'>[", SOURCE_LABELS[r$primary], "]</span>")))
      })
      return(div(class = "case-card", style = "text-align:center;",
        div(class = "badge-big", "\U0001F389"),
        h2("Case closed!"),
        div(class = "final-stat", div(class = "big", rv$score), div(class = "lbl", "points")),
        div(class = "final-stat", div(class = "big", paste0(n_src_right, "/", N_Q)),
            div(class = "lbl", "sources right (1st try)")),
        div(class = "final-stat", div(class = "big", rv$best_streak),
            div(class = "lbl", "best streak")),
        h3(badge),
        div(class = "fb fb-info", style = "text-align:left;", HTML(
          "<b>\U0001F9ED The big picture you just discovered:</b><br>
           \u2022 <b>Income Statement</b> answers: <i>How did we perform over the period?</i><br>
           \u2022 <b>Balance Sheet</b> answers: <i>What do we own and owe on one date?</i><br>
           \u2022 <b>Cash Flow Statement</b> answers: <i>Where did the cash actually go?</i><br>
           \u2022 And plenty of valuable info (stock price, forecasts, CEO pay, unit sales)
             lives <b>outside</b> these statements \u2014 knowing the limits of the statements
             is as important as knowing their contents.")),
        h4(style = "text-align:left; margin-top:22px;", "Your case file"),
        div(style = "text-align:left; max-height:260px; overflow-y:auto;
                     border:1px solid #eef1f7; border-radius:10px;", review),
        br(),
        actionButton("restart_btn", "\U0001F501 Play again", class = "btn-next")))
    }

    # ---------------- QUESTION SCREENS ----------------
    q <- cur()
    pct <- round(100 * (rv$idx - 1) / N_Q)

    div(
      div(class = "scorebar",
          span(class = "pill", paste0("Case ", rv$idx, " of ", N_Q)),
          span(class = "pill", HTML(paste0("\U0001F525 Streak: <b>", rv$streak, "</b>"))),
          span(class = "pill", HTML(paste0("\u2B50 Score: <b>", rv$score, "</b>")))),
      div(class = "progress-outer",
          div(class = "progress-inner", style = paste0("width:", pct, "%;"))),
      div(class = "case-card",
          div(class = "case-no", paste0("Case #", q$id)),
          div(class = "case-q", q$q),

          # ----- stage: pick a source -----
          if (rv$stage == "source") tagList(
            p(style = "color:#6d7f9b; margin-bottom:12px;",
              "\U0001F5C4\UFE0F Which filing cabinet holds this information?"),
            div(class = "cabinet-row",
                cabinet_btn("IS"), cabinet_btn("BS"),
                cabinet_btn("CF"), cabinet_btn("NO")),
            feedback_box(),
            div(style = "margin-top:14px;",
                actionButton("hint_btn", "\U0001F4A1 Need a clue?",
                             class = "btn-hint"))
          ),

          # ----- stage: type the number -----
          if (rv$stage == "number") tagList(
            feedback_box(),
            div(class = "num-zone",
                if (q$n_needed == 2)
                  tags$label("Type BOTH numbers (in $ millions), e.g. 112010 and 93736")
                else
                  tags$label("Type the amount (in $ millions), e.g. 416161 or 416,161"),
                textInput("num_answer", NULL, value = "",
                          placeholder = if (q$n_needed == 2)
                            "2025 number and 2024 number..." else "Your number..."),
                actionButton("submit_num", "\U0001F50E Check my find",
                             class = "btn-go"))
          ),

          # ----- stage: why-not challenge -----
          if (rv$stage == "whynot") tagList(
            feedback_box(),
            div(class = "num-zone",
                tags$label("\U0001F914 Bonus round: WHY isn't this in the statements?"),
                div(class = "whynot-opt",
                    lapply(1:3, function(i)
                      actionButton(paste0("whynot_", i),
                                   q$whynot_options[rv$whynot_order[i]]))))
          ),

          # ----- stage: reveal / move on -----
          if (rv$stage == "reveal") tagList(
            feedback_box(),
            div(style = "text-align:center;",
                actionButton("next_btn",
                             if (rv$idx < N_Q) "Next case \u27A1\UFE0F"
                             else "\U0001F3C1 See my results",
                             class = "btn-next")))
      )
    )
  })
}

shinyApp(ui, server)
